content.full_text:
January 23, 1990

Kathy Donovan
Legal Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: New Requirements Under Conflict of Interest Disclosure Provisions

Dear Ms. Donovan:

Recent passage of Assembly Bill 941 (Lampert) which adds "All Other Public Officials who manage public investments" to Section 87200, placing these officials under the broad disclosure provisions of Chapter 7, Article 2 of the act.

In accordance with California Probate Code 7640, Duty to Deposit Estate Funds, the Fresno County Public Administrator-Coroner, an elected official, upon receipt, deposits all money of the private individual decedent's estate in an insured account in a bank or savings and loan institution. Since the financial institutions which accept these deposits do not classify these deposits as public funds (that requires a special collateral insurance provision), we are under the impression that the Fresno County Public Administrator-Coroner does not manage the investments of public funds and, therefore, does not qualify as a required filer under Section 87200.

The issue in question is whether the decedent's individual estate funds are public funds as outlined in Section 87200. In order to clarify Section 87200 filing criteria for the Fresno County Public Administrator and his designee, we are requesting your office provide a definition to the estate fund classification in regard to private versus public funds in reference to Government Code Section 87200.

Kathy Donovan  
January 23, 1990

Page Two

Your assistance in this matter at your earliest convenience is greatly appreciated.

Very truly yours,

OFFICE OF DAVID M. HADDEN, M.D.
FRESNO COUNTY PUBLIC ADMINISTRATOR-CORONER/PUBLIC GUARDIAN

Tan Koo
Public Administrator-Coroner Business Manager

TK:sp

3918E
